Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: FRD3 is a key regulator of shoot mineral ion homeostasis; mutations affect multiple nutritionally important minerals simultaneously; relevant to biofortification strategies but with trade-offs (increased Mn/Co but decreased Fe/Mo); demonstrates ionomic profiling as a tool for identifying mineral nutrition genes
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: The frd3-5 allele (7-bp deletion) causes semidominant altered mineral ion profile: Mn +208%, Co +165%, Fe -26%, Mo -41% in shoots. Homozygous frd3-3 shows Mn +259%, Co +232%, Fe -27%, Mo -42%, Cd +24%.
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Forward genetic screen of fast neutron-mutagenized M2/M3 populations; complementation test (cross of 131:36 × frd3-2 confirmed same complementation group); gene cloning and sequencing revealing 7-bp deletion; Fe(III) chelate reductase activity assay; segregation analysis in M3
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Member of the multidrug and toxin efflux (MATE) family; controls iron-deficiency responses by mediating citrate efflux into the xylem, affecting iron translocation and homeostasis of multiple mineral ions
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Fast neutron-mutagenized M2/M3 lines; mutant line 131:36 (frd3-5, new allele); frd3-3 (positive control); frd3-2 (for complementation cross)
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: Wild-type FRD3 allele (maintains normal Fe homeostasis and mineral ion balance)
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Iron, manganese, cobalt, molybdenum, cadmium (multiple mineral ions)
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Loss of FRD3 function causes constitutive iron-deficiency response leading to massively elevated Mn and Co accumulation in shoots, decreased Fe and Mo, and slightly increased Cd
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Forward genetic screen using ionomic profiling (ICP-MS elemental profiling of mutagenized population); complementation with known frd3 alleles; gene sequencing
Genes.Genetic_Population: Fast neutron-mutagenized M2 population (Col0 background), M3 families
Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions (soil-grown); phenotype also confirmed on half-strength MS plates
Genes.Key_Variant_Site: 7-bp deletion 320 bp downstream of ATG in FRD3; causes frameshift after first 108 amino acids producing 59 novel amino acids before stop codon
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: This paper is primarily a proof-of-concept for ionomic profiling as a functional genomics tool. FRD3 (frd3-5) is the only gene molecularly identified and validated among the 51 mutants isolated. The remaining 50 mutant lines have altered elemental profiles but their causal genes were not cloned in this study. The paper estimates 2-4% of the Arabidopsis genome is involved in regulating the plant ionome.
Genes.Omics_Data: ICP-MS ionomic profiling data released to public database (http://hort.agriculture.purdue.edu/Ionomics/database.asp)
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Constitutively elevated Fe(III) chelate reductase activity; semidominant inheritance pattern (heterozygotes show intermediate phenotype with Mn +15%, Co +12%)
Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MATE transporter (multidrug and toxin efflux family)
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Loss of function triggers constitutive iron-deficiency response; semidominant negative effect possibly through destabilization of an unidentified protein complex by the 59 novel amino acids in frd3-5 truncated protein
Genes.Regulatory_Relationships: FRD3--|represses--|Fe-deficiency response (loss of FRD3 constitutively activates Fe-deficiency responses including elevated Fe(III) chelate reductase activity)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A new allele frd3-5 (line 131:36) was identified through forward ionomic screening of ~6,000 fast neutron-mutagenized Arabidopsis plants using ICP-MS. 2. frd3-5 contains a 7-bp deletion causing a frameshift producing a truncated protein with 59 novel amino acids, and shows semidominant inheritance with homozygotes displaying Mn +208%, Co +165%, Fe -26%, Mo -41%. 3. Complementation crosses with frd3-2 confirmed frd3-5 belongs to the same complementation group; constitutively elevated Fe(III) chelate reductase activity was observed in ~25% of M3 progeny. 4. Elevated cobalt accumulation in frd3 mutants was a novel finding not previously reported. 5. The semidominant nature of frd3-5, unlike other frd3 alleles, may result from the 59 novel amino acids destabilizing a protein complex.
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Mean percentage change compared to wild type in shoot tissue, soil-grown plants

Title: Genomic scale profiling of nutrient and trace elements in Arabidopsis thaliana
